channel.title: Research & Development on Seb Hirsch - Motion Design & Visual Effects
channel.description: Recent content in Research & Development on Seb Hirsch - Motion Design & Visual Effects

item.title: Portfolio: Reverse Engineering Adobe Portfolio
item.description:
This project represents the transition of my professional portfolio from a proprietary platform (Adobe Portfolio) to a custom-built, static site generated with Hugo.

The Motivation

Adobe Portfolio provided a convenient starting point, but I reached the limits of its customization. As a Technical Director and Professor, I wanted a site that offered full ownership and technical flexibility.

Visual Evolution: From “Monolithic” to “Six Bars”

In these reference screenshots of the original Adobe design, we can see a Monolithic Dark approach:

item.title: Website: Reverse Engineering Adobe Portfolio
item.description:
This project represents the transition of my professional portfolio from a proprietary platform (Adobe Portfolio) to a custom-built, static site generated with Hugo.

The Motivation

Adobe Portfolio provided a convenient starting point, but I reached the limits of its customization. As a Technical Director and Professor, I wanted a site that was:

  1. Portable: Ownership over the source code and content.
  2. Fast: Optimized static delivery.
  3. Unique: A design tailored to my specific “Six Bars” visual hierarchy.

The Process

The entire site is being built by reverse engineering my previous layout and refining it through an iterative pairing process with Antigravity, an advanced AI coding assistant.

item.title: Octopus: Character Study
item.description:
A personal project dedicated to my favorite animal: the octopus. This study was a deep dive into organic modeling and technical rigging, completed in 2021.

Sculpting & Modeling

The process began in ZBrush, where I focused on capturing the unique anatomy and fluid forms of the octopus. I spent significant time sculpting the intricate details of the skin texture and the complex structure of the tentacles.

Rigging & Final Polish

After sculpting the high-poly model, I performed retopology and developed a custom rig to handle the extreme flexibility required for cephalopod movement. The goal was to create a character that felt both anatomically grounded and expressive.

item.title: Posca
item.description: A high-end CGI product lighting and shading study featuring a bottle of “Posca” (an ancient Roman drink) in a historically themed environment. This project served as a deep dive into realistic materials and lighting techniques.

item.title: Sky Lanterns
item.description: A CG simulation showcasing complex particle and lighting effects, exploring the behavior of multiple light sources in a dynamic environment.
